name: The Killers
bio: The Killers keep earning through a compact but exceptionally durable 2000s catalog, led by songs that still dominate streaming and event-driven replay.
notable_for: 2000s alternative hits with unusually strong replay and social singalong value.
career_highlight: Mr. Brightside remains one of the most resilient long-tail alternative songs of the streaming era.
revenue_drivers: Alternative-pop playlist presence sustains major recurring listening.
Social, bar, and event-driven replay reinforce long-tail demand.
Cross-generational familiarity keeps core songs visible to new listeners.

earnings.assumptions: Estimate keeps The Killers's headline range as the artist-side figure and models gross catalog, label, publishing, and writer lanes from that conservative annual range.

ownership.master_owner: Likely split across label, distributor, and artist-affiliated rights depending on recording era
ownership.publishing_owner: Writer and publisher splits materially affect final artist-side income
ownership.catalog_sale_status: No full catalog sale assumption baked into this modeled range
ownership.notes: The Killers's page should be read as modeled artist-side annual income, not a public royalty statement. Ownership and label terms can materially change take-home economics.
